task_updates.executor_notes: Changed `validateAndCoerceParams` to seed the reducer with `{ ...(params ?? {}) }`, which preserves unrecognized params while schema-defined keys are still overwritten by validated/coerced values. Extended the full `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` file to include `customSeed: 42` and verified it passes through in the result alongside coerced schema params.

Added `schemasEqual(a?, b?)` and applied it at all three schema-comparison call sites in `register()` and `registerAsync()`. Expanded the full registry test file to verify no extra notification fires for a structurally equal but referentially distinct schema, while a genuinely changed schema still triggers notification.

Imported `getEffectRegistry` into `useEffectRegistry` and guarded `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` with `if (getEffectRegistry() !== registry)`. Verified the export exists and the guard uses correct reference equality for the memoized registry instance; no dedicated automated test exists in this batch.

Changed `EffectLayerSequence` to compute `inner` and skip the extra `<Sequence from={-startFrame}>` wrapper when `startFrame === 0`, while still passing `inner` through `wrapWithEffect`. Verified the updated render path by inspection; no dedicated automated test exists in this batch.

Re-ran the full `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` file successfully, then ran the broader `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest module successfully (`23` files, `82` tests). Created a throwaway repro test under `src/tools/video-editor/` that directly exercised pass-through params, structural schema dedup, registry guard idempotency, and startFrame=0 Sequence skipping; it passed and was then deleted.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Starting the reducer from `{ ...(params ?? {}) }` preserves unknown keys, and later reducer assignments still overwrite known schema keys with validated/coerced values.

Yes. `schemasEqual` is used in `register`, the `registerAsync` early return, and the `registerAsync` pending staleness check. The updated test covers both the structural-equality no-fire case and the genuinely changed schema fire case.
Yes. `getEffectRegistry` is already exported from the effects index, and `getEffectRegistry() !== registry` is the correct identity guard because `registry` comes from `useMemo`.
Yes. `inner` is what gets passed to `wrapWithEffect`, and there is no remaining code path in `EffectLayerSequence` that depends on always rendering a zero-offset `Sequence` wrapper.

Yes. All existing tests in `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` passed, including the new structural-dedup and pass-through-param coverage, and the broader `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest module also passed with no regressions. The throwaway repro test independently exercised all four fixes and passed before removal.
